title: Brewed iced tea — Black tea base (shaken)
steps: Add 300ml tea, 1 scoop of ice, and sugar to the shaker.
Add 3–5 ice cubes to the serving cup.
Shake well; strain into the serving cup.
Top off with additional brewed tea if needed to fill the cup.

Key ingredients/components: 300ml brewed tea, 1 scoop ice in shaker, sugar to spec, 3–5 ice cubes in cup.

title: Au lait (milk) — Black tea layered pour
steps: Mix 150ml milk and sugar in the Pyrex; stir well.
Add milk and ice to the serving cup up to the top of the right mountain logo (if no topping, do not exceed 200ml milk).
Slowly pour tea along the back of a spoon to create layers.

Key ingredients/components: 150ml milk, sugar, ice, brewed tea for layering.

title: Mosa Signature — Grapefruit Bloom
ingredients: grapefruit spoons + tea jelly in cup

Shake well; strain into the serving cup.
Top off with brewed tea if needed to fill the cup.

title: Matcha concentrate (bar prep)
steps: Heat water to 167°F using the kettle.
Blend 800ml of 167°F water with 35g matcha in the juice blender for 30–60 seconds.
Pour into a syrup bottle and cool with an ice water bath.
storage_life: Cool down after blending; store in syrup bottle for service (follow date/time labeling in kitchen policy).
retrieval_text: Matcha concentrate (bar prep). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to bar/kitchen-adjacent prep for service. Steps: 1) Heat water to 167°F using the kettle. 2) Blend 800ml of 167°F water with 35g matcha in the juice blender for 30–60 seconds. 3) Pour into a syrup bottle and cool with an ice water bath.

title: Matcha Series — Chestnut Forest (fixed ice)
steps: Mix 150ml milk and sugar in Pyrex (if customer requests “no cream”, use 200ml milk instead).
Add ice to the serving cup.
Slowly pour 200ml matcha along a spoon to layer.
Add ice until liquid is above the 490 line on the serving cup.
Top with chestnut cream.
retrieval_text: Matcha Series — Chestnut Forest (fixed ice). Type: drink_recipe. Applies to bar staff making drinks for customers. Key ingredients/components: 150ml milk, sugar, ice, 200ml matcha, chestnut cream.

title: Matcha Series — Genmai Matcha with Matcha Jelly
steps: Add 1 scoop Matcha Jelly to the cup.
Add 200ml Genmai (or 100ml Green + 100ml Genmai), 150ml Matcha, 1 scoop ice, and sugar to the shaker (extra matcha: adjust to 175ml Genmai / split Genmai+Green and 175ml Matcha).
Add ice to the serving cup; shake and strain.
Top off with extra tea if needed (about half Genmai and half Matcha).
retrieval_text: Matcha Series — Genmai Matcha with Matcha Jelly. Type: drink_recipe. Applies to bar staff making drinks for customers. Key ingredients/components: Matcha Jelly, Genmai, Green, Matcha, ice, sugar.

title: Matcha Series — Strawberry / Mango Matcha Latte (fixed ice)
steps: Add half spoon fresh strawberry or mango in the cup.
Mix 150ml milk with half shot strawberry syrup (or 1 shot mango syrup) and sugar in Pyrex.
Add ice to the cup to the top of the right mountain logo.
Layer in 200ml matcha with a spoon back.
Add more ice if needed to fill.
retrieval_text: Matcha Series — Strawberry / Mango Matcha Latte (fixed ice). Type: drink_recipe. Applies to bar staff making drinks for customers. title: Hot drink — Apple Spice Tea Cider / Mango Spice Tea Cider
steps: If a topping is ordered, add it first.
Add 2 shots fruit syrup, 1 shot cinnamon syrup, and black tea to a hot drink cup.
Microwave 1 minute 30 seconds to heat thoroughly.
Stir well; secure the lid.
retrieval_text: Hot drink — Apple Spice Tea Cider / Mango Spice Tea Cider. Type: drink_recipe. Applies to bar staff making drinks for customers.  Steps: 1) If a topping is ordered, add it first. 2) Add 2 shots fruit syrup, 1 shot cinnamon syrup, and black tea to a hot drink cup. 3) Microwave 1 minute 30 seconds to heat thoroughly. 4) Stir well; secure the lid. Key ingredients/components: 2 shots fruit syrup (Apple or Mango), 1 shot cinnamon syrup, black tea, microwave.

action: Before 6pm: Make 1 batch. After 6pm: Make 1/2 batch.
retrieval_text: Weekend (Friday–Sunday) kitchen prep threshold rule for Cream foams. Applies to kitchen staff. When inventory falls to about 1/4 batch volume, prep as follows. Before 6:00 PM: Make 1 batch. After 6:00 PM: Make 1/2 batch. For boba, the last batch is scheduled at 8:00 PM on weekends. These thresholds are guidance—use judgment based on the day.

title: Aluminum pots cannot be heated on the stove
entity_name: Aluminum pots cannot be heated on the stove
retrieval_text: Aluminum pots must not be heated on the stove; use them only to store tea. If cooking more than one batch at a time, use the large stainless-steel pot. Applies to kitchen staff preparing brewed tea.

title: Label containers with date and time; move date marker when refilling
entity_name: Label containers with date and time; move date marker when refilling
retrieval_text: Put the date and time on containers when storing prepared ingredients. When refilling a dispenser from a container, transfer the date/time marker to the dispenser. Applies to kitchen prep and storage.

title: Cooling Black Tea, Genmai, Barley, and Buckwheat after brewing
entity_name: Cooling Black Tea, Genmai, Barley, and Buckwheat after brewing
retrieval_text: Cool-down method for Black Tea, Genmai, Barley, and Buckwheat: place a metal container filled with ice and water on top of the tea to help it cool. Follow this when room-temperature cooling is required after brewing.

title: Crushed pistachio preparation (food processor)
retrieval_text: Crushed pistachio topping: use the food processor on Chop mode for 6–8 passes. Applies to kitchen staff preparing drink toppings.

title: Boba — rice cooker (1 batch)
ingredients: Water to 20-cup line in rice cooker
steps: Pour hot water into the rice cooker.
Bring water to a boil (about 8 minutes). When boiling, steam will come from the vent hole.
Add boba; stir to prevent sticking to the bottom.
Cook 30 minutes with heat on.
Remove from stove; rest covered 15 minutes.
Drain and rinse thoroughly under cold water to remove starchy liquid.
Add 120g sugar syrup and 250ml hot water; mix well.
retrieval_text: Boba — rice cooker (1 batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen batch recipe: cook boba in the rice cooker for drinks. After cooking, rinse, sweeten, and hold for service. Shelf life after prep: use within about 4–6 hours. Key ingredients: Boba 600g, Water to 20-cup line in rice cooker, Sugar syrup 120g, Hot water (boil), 250ml hot water for finishing, 120g sugar syrup for finishing mix. Storage/shelf-life note: Use within 4–6 hours (max).

title: Boba — pot method (1 batch)
steps: Pour hot water into the pot and bring to a boil.
Follow the same cook/rest/rinse/sweeten sequence as the rice cooker method (steps 3–7 of the rice cooker procedure).
retrieval_text: Boba — pot method (1 batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen batch recipe: alternate boba cooking method using a pot with measured water. Finish with syrup mix consistent with the rice cooker procedure. Hold time: use within about 4–6 hours. Key ingredients: Boba 600g, Water 5000ml, Sugar syrup 120g. Storage/shelf-life note: Use within 4–6 hours (max).

title: TGY Tea Jelly (1 batch)
steps: Boil the tea.
Whisk together tea, jelly powder, sugar syrup, and powdered sugar until uniform.
When boiling, pour into the metal topping container and refrigerate at least 2 hours to set.
retrieval_text: TGY Tea Jelly (1 batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen batch recipe: TGY tea jelly for toppings. Must cool/set in the refrigerator for at least two hours. Stored jelly: use within about three days. Key ingredients: TGY tea 3000ml, Jelly powder 200g, Sugar syrup 140g, Powdered sugar 100g. Storage/shelf-life note: Refrigerate; use within 3 days (max).

title: Matcha Jelly (1 batch)
ingredients: Water 3000ml total (600g at 167°F for matcha liquid; 2400ml boiled for jelly liquid)
steps: Matcha liquid: fully mix matcha powder with 600g of 167°F water from the kettle.
Jelly liquid: boil 2400ml water; mix with jelly powder and powdered sugar; must return to a full boil.
Off heat, fully combine matcha liquid and jelly liquid.
Pour into the metal topping container and refrigerate at least 2 hours to set.
retrieval_text: Matcha Jelly (1 batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen batch recipe: matcha jelly. Critical detail: the jelly liquid must reboil after adding powders/sugar. Chill until set (≥2 hours). Use within about three days. Key ingredients: SENCHA matcha powder 60g, Water 3000ml total (600g at 167°F for matcha liquid; 2400ml boiled for jelly liquid), Powdered sugar 450g, Jelly powder 180g. Storage/shelf-life note: Refrigerate; use within 3 days (max).

title: Hun Kue (1 batch)
steps: Bring water to a boil.
With heat on, whisk in HK powder and powdered sugar until dissolved.
Add syrup; continue heating until the mixture boils again.
Pour into the metal topping container; refrigerate at least 2 hours to set.
retrieval_text: Hun Kue (1 batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen batch recipe: Hun Kue jelly topping. Must reach a second boil after adding syrup. Chill until set. Use within about three days. Key ingredients: Water 3000ml, Cake jelly (HK) powder 300g, Brown sugar syrup 120g, Powdered sugar 120g. Storage/shelf-life note: Refrigerate; use within 3 days (max).

title: Brown Sugar Cream foam (1 batch)
steps: Mix milk, cream, and powder: start low speed level 2 for 2 minutes, then high speed level 10 for 8 minutes until milk foam forms (~10 minutes total).
Add brown sugar syrup; mix until fully combined.
Wrap blender with plastic wrap (about 1.5–2 feet) to prevent splashes.
retrieval_text: Brown Sugar Cream foam (1 batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen batch recipe: brown sugar cream foam. Mixer speeds/timings are specified to build stable foam. Shelf life: use the same day (about one day max). Key ingredients: Milk 500g, Cream 125g, Cream powder 125g, Brown sugar syrup 37g, Powdered sugar 37g. Storage/shelf-life note: Use within 1 day (max).

title: Pistachio Cream foam (1 batch)
steps: Mix milk, cream, and powder: start low speed level 2 for 2 minutes, then high speed level 10 for 8 minutes until milk foam forms (~10 minutes total).
Add pistachio paste; mix until fully combined.
Wrap blender with plastic wrap (about 1.5–2 feet) to prevent splashes.
retrieval_text: Pistachio Cream foam (1 batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen batch recipe: pistachio cream foam. Follow the specified mixing speeds/times, then incorporate pistachio paste. Shelf life: use the same day (about one day max). Key ingredients: Milk 550g, Cream 120g, Cream powder 125g, Powdered sugar 75g, Pistachio paste 125g. Storage/shelf-life note: Use within 1 day (max).

title: Roasted pistachio (prep)
ingredients: One layer of pistachios on a baking tray
steps: Roast in the oven at 350°F for 15 minutes; preheat the oven for 5 minutes first.
storage_life: Store and use per daily prep needs (roast as needed).
retrieval_text: Roasted pistachio (prep). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen prep: roast pistachios for toppings—single layer on a tray, 15 minutes at 350°F, with a 5-minute preheat. Key ingredients: One layer of pistachios on a baking tray. Storage/shelf-life note: Store and use per daily prep needs (roast as needed).

title: Pistachio paste (batch)
steps: Combine roasted and raw pistachios in a food processor; blend until paste consistency (about 10 processing cycles).
storage_life: Store covered; use for daily drink prep.
retrieval_text: Pistachio paste (batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen batch prep: pistachio paste from equal parts roasted and raw pistachio processed to a smooth paste. Key ingredients: Roasted pistachio 500g, Raw pistachio 500g. Storage/shelf-life note: Store covered; use for daily drink prep.

title: Chestnut Cream (1 batch)
steps: Add all ingredients to a mixing bowl.
Blend with a hand blender ~2 minutes to soft peaks; check every ~30 seconds.
retrieval_text: Chestnut Cream (1 batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen batch recipe: chestnut cream topping. Blend carefully to soft peaks. Use within about one day. Key ingredients: Cream 250g, Milk 50g, Chestnut puree 60g. Storage/shelf-life note: Use within 1 day (max).

title: Black Tea (brew batch)
steps: Heat water from the water heater and boil on the stove.
Remove from heat; add tea leaves; steep 11 minutes covered.
Strain; uncover and cool to room temperature (no ice; do not over-cool).
storage_life: Hold/use window after brewing: about 4–6 hours.
retrieval_text: Black Tea (brew batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen tea brewing: black tea batch with specified leaf weight and steep time. Cool naturally without icing. Intended use window after brewing is about 4–6 hours. Key ingredients: Tea 140g, Hot water 6000ml. Storage/shelf-life note: Hold/use window after brewing: about 4–6 hours.

title: Green Tea (brew batch)
steps: Use water at 85°C (185°F). If hotter, cool; if cooler, heat to target on the stove.
Steep 3 minutes 10 seconds covered.
Strain and mix thoroughly with ice.
storage_life: Hold/use window after brewing: about 4–6 hours.
retrieval_text: Green Tea (brew batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen tea brewing: green tea requires ~185°F water and a precise steep time, then ice-quench mixing. Use within about 4–6 hours after brewing. Key ingredients: Tea 160g, Hot water 4200ml, Ice 2800g. Storage/shelf-life note: Hold/use window after brewing: about 4–6 hours.

title: Four Seasons Tea (brew batch)
steps: Boil water from the water heater on the stove.
Remove from heat; steep 10 minutes covered.
Strain and mix thoroughly with ice.
storage_life: Hold/use window after brewing: about 4–6 hours.
retrieval_text: Four Seasons Tea (brew batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen tea brewing: Four Seasons tea batch with 10-minute steep and ice mixing. Use within about 4–6 hours. Key ingredients: Tea 160g, Hot water 4200ml, Ice 2800g. Storage/shelf-life note: Hold/use window after brewing: about 4–6 hours.

title: TGY Oolong Tea (brew batch)
steps: Boil water from the water heater on the stove.
Remove from heat; steep 6 minutes covered.
Strain and mix thoroughly with ice.
storage_life: Hold/use window after brewing: about 4–6 hours.
retrieval_text: TGY Oolong Tea (brew batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen tea brewing: TGY oolong batch with 6-minute steep and ice mixing. Use within about 4–6 hours. Key ingredients: Tea 160g, Hot water 4200ml, Ice 2800g. Storage/shelf-life note: Hold/use window after brewing: about 4–6 hours.

title: Genmai (brew batch)
steps: Boil water on the stove.
Remove from heat; add Genmai; steep 40 minutes covered.
Strain; uncover and cool naturally (no ice).
retrieval_text: Genmai (brew batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen tea brewing: Genmai batch with long steep and room-temperature cooling. Refrigerated shelf life is about three days; taste daily for quality. Key ingredients: Genmai 120g, Hot water 6000ml. Storage/shelf-life note: Refrigerated hold: about 3 days.

title: Barley tea (brew batch)
steps: Boil water on the stove.
Remove from heat; steep barley 90 minutes covered.
Strain; uncover and cool naturally (no ice).
retrieval_text: Barley tea (brew batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen tea brewing: barley tea with a 90-minute steep and room-temperature cooling. Refrigerated shelf life is about 4–5 days. Key ingredients: Barley 240g, Hot water 6000ml. Storage/shelf-life note: Refrigerated hold: about 4–5 days.

title: Buckwheat tea (brew batch)
steps: Boil water on the stove.
Cook buckwheat 5 minutes covered.
Remove from heat; steep 35 minutes covered.
Strain; uncover and cool naturally (no ice).
retrieval_text: Buckwheat tea (brew batch). Type: batch_prep_recipe. Applies to kitchen staff making drink ingredients. Kitchen tea brewing: buckwheat tea uses a cook+steep sequence then cooling. Refrigerated shelf life is about three days. Key ingredients: Buckwheat 120g, Hot water 6000ml. Storage/shelf-life note: Refrigerated hold: about 3 days.

title: Front-of-house employee expectations
retrieval_text: Front-of-house expectations at Mosa Tea: open and close the bar and front areas (see opening/closing checklists); keep the front and bathroom clean all day; take orders and prepare drinks; monitor stock and decide whether to prep more or mark items sold out for the day; support the kitchen and restock during downtime.

title: Kitchen employee expectations
retrieval_text: Kitchen expectations at Mosa Tea: prepare daily ingredients (teas, cream foams, toppings); keep the workspace clean—wash equipment after use, wipe surfaces, clean the boba sink with Simple Green daily after use; verify ingredients are ready for the next opening shift (pistachio paste, roasted pistachios, Genmai, Buckwheat Barley, toppings like tea jelly and Hun Kue, and cut fruits including apple, orange, lemon, strawberry, mango); support the front during rushes.

title: Shared teamwork expectations across roles
retrieval_text: Employees share responsibilities with their shift partner: if you are on front, check in with kitchen (and vice versa) on how to help. Additional downtime guidance is referenced under “What to do if you have down time.”

title: First day onboarding — uniform, locker, clock-in
rules: Uniform is stored in the employee locker; stay in full uniform during the shift.
Personal items may be stored in the locker; you may bring your own lock.
Clock in using Square POS; you may clock in up to 5 minutes before the scheduled start.
retrieval_text: First day at Mosa: find your uniform in the employee locker and wear full uniform on shift. You may store personal items in lockers and may use your own lock. Clock in on the Square POS (may clock in up to five minutes early). Training is supported by the teammate working with you.

title: Where to find front supplies (front cabinets)
retrieval_text: Front cabinets contain receipt/ticket rolls; cups, sample cups, lids; bags; straws; merchandise, business cards, tape; napkins and hand towels. If supplies are almost out, notify Dennis or Anna.

title: Where to find back-storage supplies
retrieval_text: Back storage guidance: boba, lychee jelly, brown sugar, creamer appear in Possimei white packages; fruit syrups, sugar, matcha powder, and grapefruit cans are label-driven; cups are in boxes marked U-600 PP; straws are 6mm (thin) vs 12mm (thick) per box labeling; cup sealer location per storage layout.

title: 4-cup trays — unboxing and shelf stocking
retrieval_text: 4-cup trays arrive in box cubes by the wall. After opening a box, remove remaining trays to the shelf. By end of closing, remove empty boxes and take them out with trash.

title: Square POS — find your 4-digit POS passcode
steps: Open Square Team app → Me (bottom).
Open Account under My Info.
Your POS passcode appears at the bottom.
retrieval_text: How to find your Square POS passcode: in the Square Team app, tap Me, then Account under My Info; the passcode is listed at the bottom.

title: Square POS — clock in, clock out, and breaks
steps: On the Square tablet, open View Time Clock.
Enter your POS passcode.
Choose clock in or clock out.
For breaks: use Start break / End break when applicable.
retrieval_text: Square time clock procedure: on the Square tablet choose View Time Clock, enter your POS passcode, then clock in/out. Breaks can be tracked with start/end break actions.

title: Square Team — view schedule and filter to my shifts
steps: Open Square Team app → Schedule (bottom).
Use the top-right menu → My shifts only to filter.
retrieval_text: Viewing shifts: Square Team app → Schedule shows who you work with. Use the menu’s “my shifts only” filter to see just your shifts.

title: Square Team — request time off
steps: Square Team → Me → Time Off.
Enter days you cannot work if not already reflected in availability.
rules: Time off requests are approved by Anna.
retrieval_text: Requesting time off in Square Team: Me → Time Off; enter unavailable days. Requests are ultimately approved by Anna.

title: Square Team — update weekly availability
steps: Square Team → Me → Availability; set each weekday.
rules: Availability repeats weekly; update it whenever your ongoing schedule needs to change.
retrieval_text: Changing availability: Square Team → Me → Availability, set each day. This pattern repeats weekly—update it if your standing schedule changes.

title: Square Team — shift cover and shift trade
steps: Under Schedule, open a shift → Request shift cover (open offer) OR Trade shift (specific teammate).
retrieval_text: Shift coverage: from Schedule, request shift cover to offer a shift broadly, or trade shifts with a specific coworker.

title: Square Team — correct clock-out time and add tips (retro edit)
steps: Square Team → Me → Hours Worked.
Select the day; adjust clock-out time and add tips if needed.
Submit request changes with a reason (for example forgot to add tips).
retrieval_text: Retroactive time corrections: Square Team → Me → Hours Worked → pick day → adjust clock-out and tips → request changes with a reason.

title: Hungry Panda — enter order naming and payment workflow
steps: Accept the Hungry Panda order; receipt prints.
Enter the same order on the POS named HP + last 4 digits of the receipt (example: HP 1234).
Pay in cash so tickets print, then refund the order with Hungry Panda as the refund reason (note HP/Panda).
Use printed tickets to make drinks.
retrieval_text: Hungry Panda procedure: accept the order, then mirror it on the POS as HP + last four digits of the receipt. Use cash pay to print tickets, refund with a Hungry Panda reason, then fulfill using printed tickets.

title: Mark items sold out in Square (include end time); toppings end-of-day restock
steps: POS → Availability → select item → Available → switch to Unavailable and set an end time (often end of day).
For toppings like boba or cream foams, return availability at end of day when restocked.
rules: Marketplace orders: also mark sold out separately on the Hungry Panda app when needed.
retrieval_text: Sold-out workflow: in Square Availability, set items unavailable with an end time. Toppings often need to be flipped back in stock at end of day. Hungry Panda may require separate sold-out marking on its app. DoorDash/Uber Eats/Grubhub/Square Online orders are handled differently—follow current third-party guidance in-store.

title: Dress code and personal presentation
rules: Wear cap and apron at all times.
Closed-toe shoes only (no sandals/flip-flops).
Nails clean; no nail polish; acrylic/spa nails require gloves always.
Long hair tied back.
retrieval_text: Dress code: cap and apron always; closed-toe shoes; hygiene maintained; no nail polish; acrylic/spa nails require gloves; long hair tied back.

title: Hygiene — handwashing and illness policy
rules: Wash hands with soap and dry with paper towels before drink making; rewash after restroom use before returning to bar/kitchen.
Use PPE (gloves) when needed.
Employees with contagious illness must take leave and notify management if unwell.
retrieval_text: Hygiene expectations: proper handwashing before drink prep; after restroom visits wash again before returning to food areas; use gloves when needed; do not work sick with contagious illnesses—notify management.

title: Food safety — equipment use and restricted areas
rules: Follow local health regulations and Mosa policies.
Report unsafe conditions immediately (example: electrical hazard).
Store equipment is only for drink preparation.
Only active employees may be in kitchen, bar, and back storage areas.
Outside food/drinks are not allowed in kitchen/bar; store personal items in back storage for breaks; do not eat in kitchen/bar areas.
retrieval_text: Food safety: comply with local health rules; report hazards; use tools only for drink prep; restrict kitchen/bar/back storage to active employees; keep outside food and eating out of kitchen/bar operational zones.

title: Equipment operation and liability reminder
retrieval_text: Operate equipment per instructions. Mosa Tea disclaims liability for injuries from misuse, negligence, or misconduct.

title: Customer service — remakes and escalation
rules: If a customer requests a remake, ask politely for the reason and remake as appropriate.
Report unusual or questionable situations to Anna.
retrieval_text: Remakes: ask politely why the guest wants a remake, then remake using good judgment; escalate odd situations to Anna.

title: Valet parking coordination (weekend nights)
rules: Godfather Restaurant valet is usually present Friday and Saturday nights.
You may move the cone as needed.
For hesitant new valet staff, offer a milk tea and remind them customers may briefly move the cone to park and should replace it after pickup.
retrieval_text: Valet coordination: weekend nights often have valet; cones may be moved for flow; if valet staff hesitate, offer a milk tea and clarify the parking agreement and cone etiquette.

title: Team communication — lateness, handoffs, group chat announcements
rules: Notify your shift partner if you will be more than 5 minutes late.
Share locations, low stock, and issues before shift handoff.
Read group chat announcements within the first 10 minutes of your shift.
retrieval_text: Team norms: notify partner if late >5 minutes; communicate stock/location/issues at handoffs; read group chat announcements early in the shift.

title: General expectations — punctuality, parking, scheduling discipline
rules: Arrive on time; park behind the plaza when possible.
Update availability weekly by Sunday for the upcoming week.
Work published shifts unless exception (emergency/doctor’s note).
retrieval_text: Scheduling: be on time; update availability weekly by Sunday; work assigned shifts or find coverage except for emergencies/documentation.

title: California break rules (summary) + Square break clocking
rules: Shifts over 6 hours: one unpaid 30-minute meal break.
Shorter shifts / every ~4 hours: one paid 10-minute rest break (per stated handbook framing).
Clock breaks in Square; breaks are required.
retrieval_text: Break policy summary (handbook): meal and rest break rules align with California labor law as described internally; employees must clock breaks in Square and take required breaks.

title: Timekeeping, lockers, employee drink benefits
rules: Clock in/out every shift.
Lockers in back storage for personal belongings; bring your own lock if desired.
One free drink per shift (same-day), plus 10 discounted drinks/month at 10% off.
retrieval_text: Timekeeping and perks: clock all shifts; store belongings in lockers; employee drink benefit is one free drink per shift (same day) and ten drinks/month at 10% off.

title: Catering — large-batch milk tea formula (pitcher + electric whisk)
steps: Example: 8 cups of Four Seasons milk tea, 100% ice, 50% sugar → 2400mL tea + 16 scoops creamer + hit 100% sugar four times into pitcher; whisk and pour to cups.
rules: Each pitcher can make about 8–10 drinks (3.5L max).
Catering formula: (300mL tea × N) + (2 scoops creamer × N) + (sugar amount × N), where N is drink count.
100% ice assumption may require tea adjustments based on ice/toppings.
storage_life: Prep timing relative to pickup; follow overnight tea rules in the catering section.
retrieval_text: Catering milk tea batching: scale with N drinks using 300mL tea per drink, 2 scoops creamer per drink, and scaled sugar; mix in pitchers with an electric whisk. Pitchers hold ~8–10 drinks (3.5L max). Example given for eight 4S milk teas at 100% ice and 50% sugar.

title: Catering — which teas may be overnight vs must be fresh
rules: Overnight teas may be used for milk teas and fruit teas, and for pure tea at 50%+ sugar.
Pure tea under 50% sugar must use fresh tea made that day.
retrieval_text: Catering tea usage: overnight tea is OK for milk teas, fruit teas, and purer teas at 50% sugar or more; purer teas below 50% sugar require fresh tea made the same day.

title: Catering / events — packing timing and materials
steps: Start packing about 1 hour before pickup.
Include straws.
Prefer fruit syrup boxes, lids, and boba/lychee jelly boxes as drink carriers; open boxes if needed.
retrieval_text: Packing catered orders: begin ~1 hour before pickup; include straws; use sturdy boxes when possible.

title: Influencer visit procedure (free drinks)
rules: You will know the IG account and arrival time in advance.
Provide 5–6 free menu drinks; influencer may choose or you may pick Mosa Signatures.
retrieval_text: Influencers: visits are pre-announced with account and time; offer 5–6 free on-menu drinks chosen by the influencer or by staff from Mosa Signatures.

title: Foodielab schedule overview (bentos + drink pickups)
retrieval_text: Foodielab overview: Tuesdays–Wednesdays bentos drop off; Thursdays–Saturdays they pick up 10 drinks for farmers market; see detailed bento and pickup instructions elsewhere in the handbook.

title: Foodielab bento combo — in-store Tuesdays & Wednesdays
rules: Place Foodielab poster by the register.
Foodielab delivers prepaid bentos and customer list by 12:30 PM.
Verify customer name on pickup.
Combo is one bento + one drink: black milk tea with boba OR orange green tea / Four Seasons tea (no substitutions).
Extra bentos for sale: customers Zelle Foodielab via QR on poster; first come first served.
Store bentos refrigerated (meat); microwave on request.
retrieval_text: Foodielab bento pickup days: poster placement, verify names from the list, combo drink options are fixed, extra sales via Zelle/QR, refrigerate meat bentos, microwave if requested.

title: Foodielab farmers market drink pickup (Thu–Sat)
rules: Make 10 drinks/day at 100% sugar and 25% ice: 7 boba milk teas and 3 orange green / Four Seasons.
Use leftover tea from prior day if available for these 10 drinks.
Pickup times: Thu 1:00 PM, Fri 1:00 PM, Sat 10:30 AM; if boba is not ready by Sat 10:30, substitute lychee jelly or tea jelly.
retrieval_text: Farmers market drink batch for Foodielab: ten drinks with specified ratios and pickup times; Saturday early pickup allows jelly substitutions if boba is not ready.

title: Tracy (@tracysgarbage) monthly event setup
steps: First Wednesday monthly, 6–9 PM collaboration.
Move two tables from storage (microwave-on-top tables).
Move a gray ottoman/bench to the front for setup.
retrieval_text: Tracy collaboration nights: monthly first Wednesday 6–9pm; relocate specified tables and ottoman/bench for her portrait setup.

title: Troubleshooting — kiosk / boba machine power cycling basics
rules: If kiosk or boba machine is off: shut down first, then plug/unplug.
Kiosk must be on the kiosk stand to work.
retrieval_text: Basic troubleshooting: for kiosk/boba machine power issues, power cycle safely; kiosk must remain on its stand to function.

title: Troubleshooting — sugar machine errors and deep clean cap
steps: Ensure full refill; toggle warmer/power; unplug briefly while on and retry.
If stuck: remove inner silver cap with gloves, rinse hot, dry with clean paper towel, reinstall; repeat until normal.
retrieval_text: Sugar syrup machine issues: verify refill and power cycles; if jammed, clean the internal silver cap with gloves using hot water, avoiding contamination.

title: Troubleshooting — Square support phone number
rules: Square support: 1-855-700-6000 (as listed in handbook).
retrieval_text: If Square hardware/software fails in ways you cannot resolve, call Square support at 1-855-700-6000.

title: Pest sighting response (roach/rat) + notify Anna
rules: Kill the pest and bleach-clean the spot.
Place pest control balls in a fabric bag in a corner.
Notify Anna about sightings/infestations.
retrieval_text: Pest protocol: kill/clean with bleach; place pest balls per handbook; escalate to Anna.

title: Health inspector visit — call Anna and milk/cream temperature
rules: Call Anna immediately if inspectors arrive.
Milk/heavy cream must pass temperature checks (handbook: target 2–4°C); cool in freezer if out of range.
retrieval_text: Health inspections are unannounced: call Anna immediately. Ensure milk/cream are in required cold range (handbook notes 2–4°C); use freezer to recover out-of-range product.

title: What to do during downtime (front bar)
steps: Prep Hungry Panda bags/trays.
Weekend/rush prep: make 3 matchas when appropriate.
Cut fruit as needed (strawberries/mangos).
Refill low syrups; restock sugar, creamer, ice, towels, soap, straws, cups.
Prepare samples; help kitchen; do dishes.
Check open Buckwheat Barley and Genmai in fridge daily: taste for quality; they last ~2–3 days and look/smell bad when spoiled.
retrieval_text: Downtime checklist for front: prep third-party materials, anticipate matcha on weekends, maintain fruit and syrups, restock disposables, support kitchen, monitor Genmai/Buckwheat quality in the fridge.

title: Tips to close earlier — bathroom restock and cold storage
steps: Refill bathroom supplies during downtime.
Refrigerate syrups and slower-moving items.
Fix the sealer; stage dirty equipment to the back.
Clean kitchen areas including boba sink, fruit cutter, and equipment washing.
retrieval_text: Closing efficiency tips: keep bathroom stocked during downtime; cold-store syrups; maintain sealer; stage washing; deep-clean key stations including boba sink and fruit cutter.

title: Yellow (cleanest towel)
rules: Use the yellow towel to wipe the rim of drink cups and any spilled drink on the cup before serving.
Yellow is only for ready-to-serve drinks (the cleanest towel).
retrieval_text: Yellow (cleanest towel). Type: cleaning_rule. Applies to bar and kitchen staff during cleaning and sanitizing tasks. Use the yellow towel to wipe the rim of drink cups and any spilled drink on the cup before serving. Yellow is only for ready-to-serve drinks (the cleanest towel).

title: Green (food-contact surfaces)
rules: Use the green towel to clean food-contact surfaces such as prep station counters and boba station counters.
Green is for areas that directly handle food or drink ingredients (second cleanest).
retrieval_text: Green (food-contact surfaces). Type: cleaning_rule. Applies to bar and kitchen staff during cleaning and sanitizing tasks. Use the green towel to clean food-contact surfaces such as prep station counters and boba station counters. Green is for areas that directly handle food or drink ingredients (second cleanest).

title: Blue (general cleaning)
rules: Use the blue towel for general surfaces such as marble countertops, kitchen sinks, work tables, and customer tables and chairs.
Do not use blue towels on food-contact areas.
retrieval_text: Blue (general cleaning). Type: cleaning_rule. Applies to bar and kitchen staff during cleaning and sanitizing tasks. Use the blue towel for general surfaces such as marble countertops, kitchen sinks, work tables, and customer tables and chairs. Do not use blue towels on food-contact areas.

title: Red (restroom / storage — dirtiest)
rules: Use the red towel for restroom cleaning or storage shelves.
Red towels are strictly not allowed in food or drink prep/serving areas.
retrieval_text: Red (restroom / storage — dirtiest). Type: cleaning_rule. Applies to bar and kitchen staff during cleaning and sanitizing tasks. Use the red towel for restroom cleaning or storage shelves. Red towels are strictly not allowed in food or drink prep/serving areas.

title: What to do if you are sick?
entity_name: What to do if you are sick?
rules: Contact the owner regarding how you are feeling, and then the owner will handle managing the potential cover. Paid sick leave applies
retrieval_text: What to do if you are sick?. Type: policy_rule. Extension policy note. Contact the owner regarding how you are feeling, and then the owner will handle managing the potential cover. Paid sick leave applies

title: Sick-hour accrual
rules: Employees earn paid sick leave according to California state law. Sick hours are tracked through payroll. Please notify management as early as possible if you need to call out sick. In CA → legally required (≈ 1 hour per 30 hours worked)
retrieval_text: Sick-hour accrual. Type: policy_rule. Extension policy note. Employees earn paid sick leave according to California state law. Sick hours are tracked through payroll. Please notify management as early as possible if you need to call out sick. In CA → legally required (≈ 1 hour per 30 hours worked)

title: TGY Special
steps: Add hun kue to the serving cup (add ice if requested).
In the shaker, combine 300 ml TGY tea, 2 scoops of creamer, sugar (based on selected level), and ice.
Shake well until fully mixed and smooth.
Pour and strain into the serving cup.
Top off if needed and serve.
retrieval_text: TGY Special. Type: drink_recipe. Extension recipe note. Step 1: Add hun kue to the serving cup (add ice if requested). Step 2: In the shaker, combine 300 ml TGY tea, 2 scoops of creamer, sugar (based on selected level), and ice. Step 3: Shake well until fully mixed and smooth. Step 4: Pour and strain into the serving cup. Step 5: Top off if needed and serve.

title: Wifi password
rules: located behind the boba sealing machine
retrieval_text: Wifi password. Type: policy_rule. Extension policy note. located behind the boba sealing machine

title: Paid Family Leave
rules: Handled through the State of California (EDD). Employees can apply directly through the state if eligible. Management can assist with required documentation if needed.
retrieval_text: Paid Family Leave. Type: policy_rule. Extension policy note. Handled through the State of California (EDD). Employees can apply directly through the state if eligible. Management can assist with required documentation if needed.

title: Bereavement leave
rules: handled on a case-by-case basis. Please communicate with management as soon as possible so we can support you and adjust scheduling.
retrieval_text: Bereavement leave. Type: policy_rule. Extension policy note. handled on a case-by-case basis. Please communicate with management as soon as possible so we can support you and adjust scheduling.

title: Health Insurance Benefits
rules: At this time, Mosa Tea does not provide health insurance benefits for part-time employees.
retrieval_text: Health Insurance Benefits. Type: policy_rule. Extension policy note. At this time, Mosa Tea does not provide health insurance benefits for part-time employees.

title: Employee Meal Policy
rules: Employees may bring their own meals to work. Meals must be stored in the designated fridge or approved storage area. All meals must be eaten in the back seating area only (not in the front or behind the counter). Please clean up after yourself and avoid leaving food out.
retrieval_text: Employee Meal Policy. Type: policy_rule. Extension policy note. Employees may bring their own meals to work. Meals must be stored in the designated fridge or approved storage area. All meals must be eaten in the back seating area only (not in the front or behind the counter). Please clean up after yourself and avoid leaving food out.

title: Square Refunds After Payment
steps: Refunds can be issued by anyone, include a note for a reason why

title: Override Code
steps: Contact the owner of the store (Anna) for the override code, sometimes a trusted worker has this.
